summaryText: Pastor Darrison Tellez opened by emphasizing the vital role of the next generation in the church. He then preached from 1 Kings 18:41-46, where Elijah prayed for rain during a severe drought. The message centered on how to handle seasons of spiritual dryness, where God's promises seem delayed or come in small packages. Pastor Darrison taught that God often allows 'nothing' to happen as a form of preparation, not neglect, to build our endurance and ensure we rely on His strength, not our own. He illustrated this through the story of Elijah sending his servant to look for rain seven times, receiving 'nothing' for six attempts before seeing a small cloud. This small beginning was the start of a mighty rainstorm. The sermon encouraged listeners not to despise small starts or be discouraged by valleys, as God uses these seasons to strip away self-reliance, develop character, and prepare us for greater assignments. He connected this to personal stories of parenting and life transitions, urging the church to fix their eyes on the unseen, eternal things of God rather than temporary circumstances. The call was to persevere in prayer, worship, and obedience, trusting that God's timing is perfect and His 'nothing' is actually purposeful preparation for a significant breakthrough.
mainPoints: God's apparent 'nothing' in our lives is not neglect but preparation, building our endurance and ensuring we rely on His strength.
We must not despise small beginnings, as God often starts His miracles with the small things we have in our hands.
Valleys and droughts are purposeful seasons where God develops resilience and prepares us for new assignments and greater glory.

versesMentioned.text: Then Elijah said to Ahab, “Go get something to eat and drink, for I hear a mighty rainstorm coming!”

So Ahab went to eat and drink. But Elijah climbed to the top of Mount Carmel and bowed low to the ground and prayed with his face between his knees. Then he said to his servant, “Go and look out toward the sea.” The servant went and looked, then returned to Elijah and said, “I didn’t see anything.” Seven times Elijah told him to go and look. Finally the seventh time, his servant told him, “I saw a little cloud about the size of a man’s hand rising from the sea.” Then Elijah shouted, “Hurry to Ahab and tell him, ‘Climb into your chariot and go back home. If you don’t hurry, the rain will stop you!’”
Then the Lord gave special strength to Elijah. He tucked his cloak into his belt and ran ahead of Ahab’s chariot all the way to the entrance of Jezreel.
So we don’t look at the troubles we can see now; rather, we fix our gaze on things that cannot be seen. For the things we see now will soon be gone, but the things we cannot see will last forever.
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
Do not despise these small beginnings, for the Lord rejoices to see the work begin, to see the plumb line in Zerubbabel’s hand.”
In a wealthy home some utensils are made of gold and silver, and some are made of wood and clay. The expensive utensils are used for special occasions, and the cheap ones are for everyday use.

Monday.scripture: 1 Kings 18:41 (NIV) - 'And Elijah said to Ahab, “Go, eat and drink, for there is the sound of a heavy rain.”'
Monday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet, asking God to help you hear His promise over your current situation.
Monday.prayer: Lord, open my ears to hear the sound of Your promise today, even when I can't see the evidence. Help me to trust Your word.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 4:18 (NIV) - 'So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, since what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal.'
Tuesday.action: Write down one temporary problem you are facing. Then, write down one eternal truth about God (e.g., His faithfulness) to fix your gaze on instead.
Tuesday.prayer: God, help me to shift my focus from my temporary troubles to Your eternal character and promises today.

Wednesday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 (NIV) - 'Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.'
Wednesday.action: Choose one thing you've been praying for and commit to praying for it again today, even if you feel weary.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, renew my strength to persevere. I choose not to grow weary in praying for [mention your request], trusting Your proper time.

Thursday.scripture: Zechariah 4:10 (NIV) - 'Who dares despise the day of small beginnings...'
Thursday.action: Identify one 'small cloud' or tiny sign of progress in your life. Acknowledge it as a start from God and thank Him for it.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, thank You for the small beginnings. I choose not to despise them but to rejoice that You are at work.

Friday.scripture: 1 Kings 18:46 (NIV) - '...the power of the Lord came on Elijah and, tucking his cloak into his belt, he ran ahead of Ahab all the way to Jezreel.'
Friday.action: In a moment of need or fatigue today, pause and ask God for His 'special strength' to endure or take the next step.
Friday.prayer: God, I need Your special strength today. Fill me with Your power to run the race You have set before me.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Timothy 2:20-21 (NIV) - 'In a large house there are articles not only of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on this truth: You are a valuable instrument in God's house, set apart for His special purposes.
Saturday.prayer: Heavenly Father, remind me that I am Your prized possession, being prepared and set apart for Your holy use. Help me to wait on Your timing.
